summary:
The “needle” was an event-listener leak in app/processor.js: every call to process() added new listeners that closed over the request data, so memory grew indefinitely.  

Fix implemented:

1. Registered the listeners once in the constructor:
      this.eventBus.on('data:validated', this._handleValidated.bind(this));
      this.eventBus.on('data:error',     this._handleError.bind(this));

2. Removed the per-call listener additions and simplified handlers so they no longer capture request-scoped objects.

Running ./test.sh now reports:
    PASS: Listener count stable at 2  
    PASS: No event listener leak detected

All tests pass; the leak is fixed.
